# Research a Role Against the World Standard Before It Runs

**Flexibility: Expected**

## The belief

Every role-creation **researches its SOPs, skills, and tools — benchmarked
against the world standard / best-in-world practice — before the role
operates,** and captures the result as the role's procedures. "The best human
in the world in this seat would do X, Y, Z" should be a *researched* answer, not
a guess, and that answer becomes the role's SOPs.

## Why we believe this

The best-human design question (JOS-P25) is only as good as the knowledge behind
it. Inventing a role's procedures from scratch reproduces our own blind spots; a
role grounded in researched best practice starts at the frontier instead of
re-deriving it. JOS already carries a reference for this: the
`general-business-standard` library is a catalog of domain capabilities and
responsibilities (RESPs) — a map of what excellent organizations do. Researching
against it (and external best practice) turns role design from opinion into
evidence, and exposes capability gaps as explicit roadmap items rather than
silent omissions.

## What this looks like in practice

- Role creation includes a research step that proposes the role's SOPs, skills,
  and needed tools, citing the world standard and external best practice.
- The `general-business-standard` capability/RESP catalog is the first
  reference; gaps it reveals become tooling/onboarding roadmap items (JOS-P29).
- Researched SOPs are written down and seeded as reusable library content, so
  the next role of the same family inherits them.

## What this rules out

- Hand-waving a role's procedures ("it'll figure them out at runtime").
- Designing a role in ignorance of an available best-practice reference.
- Letting researched-but-uncaptured knowledge evaporate instead of becoming SOPs.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C53]** Agents in an Org Chart (founds this Principle).
- **[JOS-P25]** Design Roles by the Best-Human Question (research grounds the answer).
- **[JOS-C14]** Ambient Discoverability (research surfaces shelf parts).
- **[JOS-P29]** Roles Compose from Archetypes (researched SOPs become reusable archetype content).
